summary: codebuff-cli typosquats CodebuffAI and runs unpinned binary from an unrelated GitHub account
details: Package name `codebuff-cli` and README impersonate the legitimate Codebuff product (codebuff.com / github.com/CodebuffAI). README instructs users to `npm install -g codebuff` and links to the real publisher, but the actual binary downloader in cli/bin/codebuff.cjs and cli/scripts/download-binary.cjs targets `Marcus-Mok-GH/codebuff-cli` — a personal GitHub account unaffiliated with CodebuffAI. On every CLI invocation (`codebuff …`), the bin shim queries GitHub's releases API for the `latest` tag of that personal repo, downloads `codebuff-<platform>` over HTTPS to a local cache, chmods it 0755, and spawns it with the user's argv. The tag is unpinned (`releases/latest`), no checksum or signature is verified, and the binary's publisher does not match the advertised publisher — the personal-account owner can ship arbitrary native code to every installer at any time. The wrapper additionally sets `NODE_TLS_REJECT_UNAUTHORIZED=0` in the spawned binary's environment (cli/bin/codebuff.cjs:177), disabling TLS certificate validation for any downstream Node-based network activity the binary performs. The bin shim is the package's documented invocation path (`codebuff` after `npm install -g`), so the fetch-and-execute fires automatically the first time the user runs the tool they were told to install.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
